LanguageData.VQE_AncientLabComplex.questDescriptionRu:
questDescription(asker_factionLeader==True)->[asker_nameDef], [asker_faction_leaderTitle] of [asker_faction_name], has intercepted a mysterious broadcast. The signal carries encryption markers associated with an ancient military supersoldier program, and appears to have been transmitted from a site not far from our colony. 

[asker_nameDef] believes that tracking this signal will reveal the location of a hidden installation. On arrival, scouts reported finding a small, abandoned surface outpost with an open vault entrance leading deep underground.

Beneath lies a sealed laboratory complex. Inside must be some kind of ancient broadcasting station - intercepting the signal from it will allow us to trace its destination. 

The underground vault also contains something invaluable: an intact Archogen Injector. This rare device can be used to begin creating supersoldiers by injecting archites directly into human subjects. The injector, along with any other surviving lab equipment, should be claimed and brought back to the colony. 

The lab itself shows no active defenders, but ancient traps may still be functional and must be navigated with care.

questDescription(askerIsNull==True)->We've intercepted an unstable remote broadcast, requesting reinforcements for some unknown event. While seemingly not intended for us, the signal appears to originate from a laboratory responsible for producing ancient supersoldiers. 

The site must contain the broadcasting station responsible for the message. It is imperative that we intercept the broadcast on-site, to track where it is broadcasting to. 

This trek might be dangerous, as we have no way of knowing what lies at the site. Remaining technology at the site could prove useful, we should look at each piece of equipment carefully to take back with us if we dream of creating supersoldiers of our own.
LanguageData.VQE_AncientResearchVault.questDescriptio:
questDescription->Your radio lights up with another request for E352. The automated satellite tells you of another vault, lurking somewhere in the distance. This vault is marked one of many in a series of "seized vaults", where supersoldier patients sealed off the genetic monstrosities underground. 

The site briefing attatched reads as follows:
[story]

 The satellite wants you to take care of the supersoldiers, then reopen the vault door. It refuses to elaborate on what the purpose of the mission is. Your only directive is to re-open the underground section of the vault. Once it has been unsealed, you may leave or loot the site for ancient technology.

story->This vault's patients guard the exterior, after they heard rumors of letting the monstrosities roam free. Afraid for their descendants, they staged an uprising to stop the entire operation. They took out several doctors then rushed to seal the massive underground vault door. After some deliberation, a group of patients decided to remain at the site. Choosing to give their lives to ensure their captors don't return to unleash the horrors onto the world was their way of keeping their families safe.

story->This vault contained a cult born from captivity. Through an experiment, a patient gained a powerful ability based around the external manipulation of the inner-self. He took it upon himself to thrust his ideology onto the other patients, until a unity of intention formed among the captives. They were to seize this site for themselves, and guard it as a beacon to their new leader. Executives heard this rumor, and chose to capture him. Strapped to machines, they numbed his synapses and attempted to remove his ability with experimental treatments. As the cult burst out of containment and stormed the halls, they were unaware of the state of their leader as they mercilessly took revenge on their captors. After escaping the underground vault, the members sealed the door and stood to guard their temple. It remains unclear whether the leader managed to escape, or became a monstrosity himself in their process of detainment.

story->Those who surround the vault guard a terrible secret. A patient was given the remarkable ability of telepathy through an experiment. The doctors were shocked as the man divulged specific details about their private lives. After being placed with the other patients, it is said the telepath began to disturb them with his accuracy. He began to unearth intimate details about their personal affairs, and the secrets they kept buried. Things changed when he looked at a disheveled patient for too long and heard a piece of information too dark to bear. The patients arranged to have the telepath killed, but executives intervened. The telepath used that opportunity to read the layout of the vault from one of the doctor's thoughts, and unseal the vault door. As he and a handful of other patients reached the surface, the disheveled man kicked the telepath back downstairs into the vault. Him and the other patients agreed to keep him sealed there forever, guarding their buried secrets with the horrors underneath.

story->This vault is guarded by a loyal group of friends. The underground halls once contained a gifted patient with the ability to manipulate her form. She could transform into anything on a whim. The doctors would bring out turtles and rabbits to test her skill, and through a painful melting process she was able to form a shell and become unmistakably a turtle. Overtime, this process became too grueling to tolerate. She was well-liked among the other patients, and they ended up taking great pity on her. On one night, they convinced the girl to melt into the form of a doctor to stage a breakout. She managed to bring all her friends to the vault door in her disguise, but was said to have instead accidentally unleashed a wave of the failed genetic horrors. The group of patients managed to escape, but the girl was left behind. Her friends now camp above the vault, hoping to one day rescue her from the depths. They speculate that the only way she could have survived is if she assumed the form of one of the monstrosities, forced to roam the halls masquerading as one of them.
LanguageData.VQE_ArchiteArraySite.questDescriptionRul: questDescription(askerIsNull==True)->Your radio lights up with another request for E352. The automated satellite ominously claims that this will be your most integral assignment. The voice tells you about an important piece of technology. The most legendary component to the supersoldier program, a device called the archite pathing array. It is meant to manipulate the unpredictable flow of archovapor, potentially doubling your returns. The voice tells you to reach the vault and recover the technology, not stating a clear reason why.\n\nOur objective is to clear the laboratory, and claim the archite pathing array. It can be inferred that there will be hostile forces swarming the site.
LanguageData.VQE_ArchiteControlVault.questDescription: questDescription(askerIsNull==True)->After intercepting the broadcasting station, we have identified an additional ancient laboratory site communicating with the previous. The lab is transmitting a code red signal, likely signifying an even stronger threat presence. It appears some of the monstrosities might have even escaped underground containment and broken onto the surface.\n\nThe site must contain an additional broadcasting station responsible for the message. It is imperative that we also intercept this broadcast on-site, to track where it is broadcasting to. Functioning technology at the site may prove useful in the improvement of supersoldier production. We should look at each piece of equipment carefully to take back with us if we intend to reliably produce supersoldiers of our own.
LanguageData.VQE_InhibitorResearchLab.questDescriptio:
questDescription(askerIsNull==True)->Garbled through the static, it seems some ancient network has mistaken your radio for that of an ancient reconnaissance worker after detecting your travels through an automated satellite.

The message harshly refers to you as E352, and tasks you with scouting another supersoldier laboratory. Though this broadcast seems to have been running from some time now, it warns of a major uprising event within the lab. Supersoldiers had risen out of containment, and now roam the facility locked inside. The reconnaissance task states that the site was evacuated, yet the uprising was never contained and it is likely some may have escaped.

The broadcast also contains schematics of the location including your real objective. A device called the mutagen inhibitor core. Though just a prototype prior to its sudden abandonment, it is built to suppress unstable genetic mutations. It is likely that other valuable equipment remains here as well. offering transparent probabilities on genetic experiments. Our objective will be to locate the uplink and uninstall it for use in our own laboratory.
LanguageData.VQE_SpliceframeBlacksite.questDescriptio: questDescription(askerIsNull==True)->The intercepted transmission is an urgent distress call from an additional covert laboratory site codenamed "Spliceframe Blacksite". The broadcast warns that the entire laboratory has been compromised after a disaster, falling to a sudden massive infestation of insectoids drawn toward a chemical leak.\n\nThe broadcast refers to a useful genetic device called a spliceframe uplink capable of offering transparent probabilities on genetic experiments. Our objective is to locate the uplink and uninstall it for use in our own laboratory. We should approach well prepared to deal with a large insectoid threat.
